description: Perovskite solar cell material database with per-parameter provenance. Every numerical value ties to a verifiable primary source. This replaces the hardcoded dataclass entries in physics/materials.py.
notes: Every parameter has: value, unit, source (short citation), DOI, method, confidence tier, notes.
Confidence tiers: HIGH (multi-source or authoritative measurement), MEDIUM (single peer-reviewed paper), LOW (single source with flags).
When a parameter has literature spread, the 'range' field lists the published min/max.
Materials previously tied only to unverified references have been marked for re-sourcing.
Optical constants (alpha_coeff) here are rough starting values. Real TMM work should pull tabulated n(λ),k(λ) from the database's /optical/ subtree.

chung_2012_jacs_cssni3.citation: Chung, Song, Im, Androulakis, Malliakas, Li, Freeman, Kenney, Kanatzidis, J. Am. Chem. Soc. 134, 8579-8587 (2012). CsSnI3: Semiconductor or Metal? Hall measurements gave hole mobility ~58 cm^2/Vs, carrier density ~10^17 cm^-3.

euvrard_2021_nrm.citation: Euvrard, Yan, Mitzi, Nature Reviews Materials (2021). Electrical doping in halide perovskites. Reports MAPbI3 and MAPbBr3 typical doping <10^14 cm^-3 from multiple measurement methods.

saidaminov_2015_natcomm_mapbbr3.citation: Saidaminov et al., Nat. Commun. 6, 7586 (2015). High-quality bulk hybrid perovskite single crystals within minutes by inverse temperature crystallization. Reports MAPbBr3 single-crystal trap density 5.8e9 cm^-3 by SCLC.

magomedov_2018_aem_sam.citation: Magomedov, Al-Ashouri, Kasparavicius, Strazdaite, Niaura, Jost, Malinauskas, Albrecht, Getautis, Adv. Energy Mater. 8, 1801892 (2018). Self-assembled hole-transporting monolayer; PCE 17.8%. SAM thickness ~1 nm — bulk transport parameters are not well-defined.

shin_2016_chemmater_cbts.citation: Shin, Saparov, Zhu, Huhn, Blum, Mitzi, Chem. Mater. 28, 4771-4780 (2016). BaCu2Sn(S,Se)4: Earth-Abundant Chalcogenides for Thin-Film Photovoltaics.

yoo_2021_nature.citation: J. J. Yoo et al., 'Efficient perovskite solar cells via improved carrier management,' Nature 590, 587 (2021)

min_2021_nature.citation: Min, Lee, Kim, Park, Hu, Lee, Kim, Choi, Kim, Cho, Kim, Choi, Hong, Park, Heo, Hwang, Choi, Bae, Kim, Hong, Seo, Park, Kim, Yang, Seok, Nature 598, 444-450 (2021). Perovskite solar cells with atomically coherent interlayers on SnO2 electrodes. NREL-certified PCE 25.7%.

haider_2018_jappa_cusbs2.citation: Haider, Anwar, Wang, J. Phys. Chem. Solids (2018). CuSbS2 thin film for solar cell applications. Standard parameter set widely used in SCAPS literature.

amu_2020_jpscond_v2o5.citation: Amu, Khatibi, Soucase, Mater. Sci. Semicond. Process. (2020). V2O5 as hole transport layer in perovskite solar cells. Standard SCAPS parameter set.

hadi_2020_solrl_in2s3.citation: Hadi, Ali, Sarker, Sol. RRL (2020). In2S3 as buffer layer for perovskite solar cells.

brivio_2014.citation: Brivio et al., Phys. Rev. B 89, 155204 (2014)

hossain_2022_acs_omega.citation: Hossain, Rubel, Toki, Alam, Rahman, Bencherif, ACS Omega 7, 43210-43230 (2022). Effect of Various Electron and Hole Transport Layers on the Performance of CsPbI3-Based Perovskite Solar Cells: A Numerical Investigation in DFT, SCAPS-1D, and wxAMPS Frameworks. Best device PCE 17.90% (ITO/TiO2/CsPbI3/CBTS/Au).
hossain_2022_acs_omega.absorber_material: CsPbI3 (cubic phase, Eg=1.694 eV input, 1.483 eV from DFT-PBE)

chabri_2023_jem.citation: Chabri, Benhouria, Oubelkacem, Kaiba, Essaoudi, Ainane, J. Electron. Mater. 52, 2722-2736 (2023). Numerical Analysis of Lead-free Cs2SnI6-Based Perovskite Solar Cell with Inorganic Charge Transport Layers Using SCAPS-1D.

eperon_2014_ees.citation: Eperon et al., Energy Environ. Sci. 7, 982 (2014)

minemoto_2015_solmat.citation: Minemoto & Murata, Sol. Energy Mater. Sol. Cells 133, 8 (2015)

zhao_2017_aem.citation: Zhao et al., Adv. Energy Mater. 7, 1700131 (2017)

araujo_2025_rsc_sust.citation: Araújo V.H.D., Nogueira A.F., Tristão J.C., dos Santos L.J., RSC Sustainability 3, 4314-4335 (2025)

snaith_2013_jpcl.citation: H. J. Snaith et al., 'Anomalous hysteresis in perovskite solar cells,' J. Phys. Chem. Lett. 5, 1511 (2014)

ponseca_2014_jacs.citation: C. S. Ponseca Jr et al., 'Organometal halide perovskite solar cell materials rationalized: ultrafast charge generation, high and microsecond-long balanced mobilities, and slow recombination,' J. Am. Chem. Soc. 136, 5189 (2014)

wang_2019_acsel_sn.citation: F. Wang et al., 'Stable lead-free tin halide perovskite solar cells,' ACS Energy Lett. 4, 222 (2019)

burschka_2013_nature.citation: J. Burschka et al., 'Sequential deposition as a route to high-performance perovskite-sensitized solar cells,' Nature 499, 316 (2013)

koh_2014_jpcc.citation: T. M. Koh et al., 'Formamidinium-containing metal-halide: an alternative material for near-IR absorption perovskite solar cells,' J. Phys. Chem. C 118, 16458 (2014)

liu_2013_planar_deposition.citation: M. Liu, M. B. Johnston, H. J. Snaith, 'Efficient planar heterojunction perovskite solar cells by vapour deposition,' Nature 501, 395 (2013)

tiep_2016_aem_ZnO.citation: N. H. Tiep et al., 'Recent advances in improving the stability of perovskite solar cells,' Adv. Energy Mater. 6, 1501420 (2016)

bi_2016_aem_spiro.citation: D. Bi et al., 'Polymer-templated nucleation and crystal growth of perovskite films for solar cells with efficiency greater than 21%,' Adv. Energy Mater. 6, 1600457 (2016)

arora_2017_science_CuSCN.citation: N. Arora et al., 'Perovskite solar cells with CuSCN hole extraction layers yield stabilized efficiencies greater than 20%,' Science 358, 768 (2017)

hou_2020_science_2pacz.citation: Y. Hou et al., 'Efficient tandem solar cells with solution-processed perovskite on textured crystalline silicon,' Science 367, 1135 (2020)

saliba_2016_ees.citation: Saliba M., Matsui T., Seo J.Y. et al., "Cesium-containing triple cation perovskite solar cells: improved stability, reproducibility and high efficiency", Energy Environ. Sci. 9, 1989-1997 (2016)
saliba_2016_ees.verification_note: Confirmed via PubMed (PMID 27478500), RSC publishing, and EPFL infoscience. Reports stabilized 21.1% PCE for triple-cation Cs/MA/FA composition.

yang_2015_science.citation: Yang W.S., Noh J.H., Jeon N.J. et al., "High-performance photovoltaic perovskite layers fabricated through intramolecular exchange", Science 348, 1234-1237 (2015)
yang_2015_science.verification_note: Confirmed via Science website. Certified 20.1% PCE via intramolecular DMSO/FAI exchange. Foundational FAPbI3 paper.

wang_2019_acsel_srv.citation: Wang, Bonoldi, Gibson, Lyu, Ho, Hueston, Marquez, Ginger, ACS Energy Lett. 4, 222-227 (2019). Reducing surface recombination velocities at the electrical contacts will improve perovskite photovoltaics. Time-resolved PL measurements of SRV on 14 transport materials.

savva_2019_solrrl_PEDOT.citation: A. Savva et al., 'PEDOT:PSS in perovskite solar cells,' Solar RRL 3, 1900033 (2019)

gloeckler_2003_wcpec.authors: Gloeckler, M.; Fahrenbruch, A. L.; Sites, J. R.
gloeckler_2003_wcpec.title: Numerical modeling of CIGS and CdTe solar cells: setting the baseline
gloeckler_2003_wcpec.venue: Proc. 3rd World Conference on Photovoltaic Energy Conversion (Osaka), vol. 1, pp. 491-494
gloeckler_2003_wcpec.notes: Canonical community baseline parameter set for CdTe (SnO2/CdS/CdTe) and CIGS SCAPS modelling; reproduced in dozens of later studies. Conference proceedings (no DOI assigned).

song_2016_vacuum.authors: Song, T.; Kanevce, A.; Sites, J. R. (parameter lineage); Ma, X. et al.
song_2016_vacuum.title: Dependence of performance parameters of CdTe solar cells on semiconductor properties studied by using SCAPS-1D
song_2016_vacuum.notes: Baseline + champion-fit CdTe SCAPS models (19.6-21%); source for parameter ranges of CdS/CdTe used to bound calibration.

kephart_2016_solmat.title: Band alignment of front contact layers for high-efficiency CdTe solar cells
kephart_2016_solmat.notes: Front-stack band alignment for CdTe; used for SnO2/CdS affinity confidence.

zyoud_2021_crystals.authors: Zyoud, S. H.; Zyoud, A. H.; Ahmed, N. M.; Abdelkader, A. F. I.
zyoud_2021_crystals.title: Numerical Modelling Analysis for Carrier Concentration Level Optimization of CdTe Heterojunction Thin Film-Based Solar Cell with Different Non-Toxic Metal Chalcogenide Buffer Layers Replacements: Using SCAPS-1D Software
zyoud_2021_crystals.notes: Peer-reviewed SCAPS-1D SnO2-class/CdS/CdTe reference: 17.43% with CdS buffer. Used as the OUT-OF-SAMPLE sanity band for the CdTe benchmark (replaces a DOI-less preprint removed in the final-edition reference audit).

Eg_eV.notes: Tetragonal phase at 300 K. Bandgap varies 1.5-1.6 eV with composition and processing.

mu_n_cm2_Vs.notes: Strongly processing-dependent; single crystals reach ~160 cm^2/V/s. Device-effective value used by the solver; the measured value (see 'measured') is higher. Separated to avoid mis-attributing the simulation value to the measurement.

mu_p_cm2_Vs.notes: Device-effective value used by the solver; the measured value (see 'measured') is higher. Separated to avoid mis-attributing the simulation value to the measurement.

Nt_bulk_cm3.method: Default polycrystalline thin-film value used in PSC device modeling. Single-crystal Shi 2015 measurements give 10^9-10^10 cm^-3 (10.1126/science.aaa2725). Polycrystalline films relevant for solar cells exhibit 10^14-10^16 cm^-3 (Euvrard 2021 review).
Nt_bulk_cm3.notes: Choice of 1e15 reflects published consensus range for thin-film MAPbI3 used in SCAPS-1D simulations.

MAPbI3.migration_note: Retained. Lost one weak citation [6] (Datto ChemNanoMat 2026 - integrity flag). All parameters now tied to verified sources.

Eg_eV.method: Hossain 2022 Table 1, CsPbI3 column. SCAPS-1D input value.

doping_cm3.notes: Acceptor density NA. Hossain Table 1 footnote (a).

Nt_bulk_cm3.notes: Bulk defect density. Hossain Table 1 footnote (a).

alpha_coeff_cm.notes: Effective absorption coefficient (Beer-Lambert proxy for Hossain's Tauc model).

mu_p_cm2_Vs.notes: Highly asymmetric mobility - known feature of vacancy-ordered double perovskites.

Eg_eV.notes: Wide-gap absorber. Used in mixed-halide alloys and as top cell in tandems.

doping_cm3.method: Undoped MAPbBr3 has carrier density <10^14 cm^-3 from multiple measurement methods (Hall, Mott-Schottky, four-point probe). Saidaminov 2015 single-crystal measurements give upper bound; thin films typically 10^13 cm^-3.

Nt_bulk_cm3.method: Saidaminov 2015 reports MAPbBr3 single-crystal trap density 5.8e9 cm^-3 by SCLC. Polycrystalline thin films relevant for solar cells exhibit 10^14-10^16 cm^-3 (one order higher than single crystal, consistent with literature).

Nt_bulk_cm3.notes: Higher than Pb perovskites due to Sn2+/Sn4+ oxidation creating shallow defects.

mu_n_cm2_Vs.method: Estimated from CsSnI3 Hall mobility 58 cm^2/Vs scaled for thin-film polycrystalline material; n-type mobility is much lower than p-type for CsSnI3.

mu_p_cm2_Vs.method: Hall effect on black orthorhombic CsSnI3 single crystals; carrier density ~10^17 cm^-3.

doping_cm3.notes: Heavy self-doping typical of Sn perovskites.

FA0.83Cs0.17Pb_I0.6Br0.4_3.category: Wide-bandgap perovskite for tandem top cells

Eg_eV.method: Tunable via I/Br ratio. 60:40 I:Br gives 1.68 eV — standard wide-gap top cell for 4T and 2T tandems.

alpha_coeff_cm.notes: Wider gap means lower absorption coefficient near band edge than MAPbI3.

FA0.83Cs0.17Pb_I0.6Br0.4_3.usage_note: Standard top cell for perovskite-silicon and all-perovskite tandems. Eg=1.68 eV gives optimal current-matching with c-Si bottom cell.

doping_cm3.notes: p-type; low doping is characteristic of as-grown CdTe

Nt_bulk_cm3.notes: Single midgap donor level in the baseline; calibrated within this published range to reproduce the baseline J-V (see benchmark C1)

alpha_coeff_cm.method: Tauc prefactor consistent with alpha~1e5 cm^-1 above gap
alpha_coeff_cm.notes: CdTe direct-gap absorption ~1e5 cm^-1; Tauc form alpha=alpha0*sqrt(E-Eg)/E

sigma_e_cm2.notes: With Nt=2e14 cm^-3 and v_th=1e7 cm/s gives tau_SRH = 0.5 ns, the community-baseline CdTe lifetime

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.
Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.
interface_srv_cm_s.notes: TiO2 — high SRV due to mid-gap defects at TiO2/perovskite interface

TiO2.migration_note: Previously tagged [3][6]. [6] was the ChemNanoMat 2026 integrity-flag entry and has been removed.

doping_cm3.notes: Typical n-doped ZnO used in PSCs. Undoped ZnO has lower carrier density (~1e16).

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.
interface_srv_cm_s.notes: ZnO — moderate SRV; chemical reactivity with MAPbI3

ZnO.known_issues: ZnO is alkaline and degrades MA-based perovskites over weeks. Fine for short-term device studies; not for stability predictions.

Eg_eV.notes: Optical gap. HOMO-LUMO gap in films; single-molecule gap is larger.

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.
interface_srv_cm_s.notes: C60 — very clean interface, well-aligned LUMO

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.

PCBM.migration_note: Previously carried [3][6]. The [6] Datto ChemNanoMat entry has been removed; all values now source to Hossain 2022 (verified GOLD-OA).

Eg_eV.notes: Indirect bandgap. Used as bottom cell in perovskite/Si tandems.

doping_cm3.notes: Typical lightly doped n-type bottom cell.

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.
interface_srv_cm_s.notes: c-Si — for tandem use; not really an ETL

c_Si.usage_note: Only relevant for tandem simulations. Do not use as ETL in a single-junction device.

Nt_cm3.notes: Defect density. Hossain 2022 Table 1 footnote (a): held constant during initial optimization.

thickness_nm_reference.notes: Reference thickness used in Hossain 2022 device D5.

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.

IGZO.usage_note: Used in benchmark device D5 (ITO/IGZO/CsPbI3/CBTS/Au, PCE 17.76%).

Eg_eV.notes: Hossain 2022 Table 1, WS2 column. Indirect gap of bulk WS2.

thickness_nm_reference.notes: Reference thickness used in Hossain 2022 device D6.

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.
interface_srv_cm_s.notes: WS2 — TMD interface, moderate SRV (limited data)

WS2.usage_note: Used in benchmark device D6 (ITO/WS2/CsPbI3/CBTS/Au, PCE 17.82%).

Eg_eV.notes: Chabri 2023 Table 1. Same as undoped ZnO; Al-doping increases conductivity, not optical gap.

doping_cm3.notes: Heavy Al-donor doping; gives degenerate n-type conductivity for use as window/contact buffer.

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.

AZO.usage_note: Window/buffer layer in benchmark device D7 (ITO/CuI/Cs2SnI6/ZnO/AZO/Ag, Chabri 2023).

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.

In2S3.usage_note: Buffer layer common in CIGS solar cells; recently explored for tin-based perovskites. Replaces toxic CdS in many architectures.

Nt_bulk_cm3.method: acceptor-type defects (baseline uses 1e18 compensating; effective recomb. level here)
Nt_bulk_cm3.notes: Baseline CdS is fully compensated (Nt = Nd = 1.1e18 scale); acceptor defects 1e18 cm^-3 per Gloeckler 2003

sigma_e_cm2.notes: CdS bulk recombination is dominated by compensation; cross-section poorly constrained experimentally, baseline value adopted

sigma_h_cm2.notes: CdS bulk recombination is dominated by compensation; cross-section poorly constrained experimentally, baseline value adopted

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.
interface_srv_cm_s.notes: Spiro-OMeTAD — high SRV (Wang 2019); affected by Li-TFSI doping

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.
Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.
Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.
Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.
interface_srv_cm_s.notes: PEDOT:PSS — high SRV; acidic interface degrades perovskite

PEDOT:PSS.known_issues: Acidic and hygroscopic. Widely used in p-i-n structures but a known degradation driver.

mu_n_cm2_Vs.notes: Effective bulk-equivalent mobility for SCAPS-1D modeling. SAMs are ~1 nm thick — bulk transport is physically ill-defined; this value is an empirical fit used in published SCAPS studies of SAM-based PSCs.
mu_n_cm2_Vs.model_caveat: Treat any simulation result involving SAMs qualitatively. The SAM is better modeled as an interface dipole than as a bulk transport layer.

mu_p_cm2_Vs.notes: Effective bulk-equivalent value for SCAPS-1D modeling. Same caveat as mu_n: SAM thickness ~1 nm makes bulk mobility ill-defined.
mu_p_cm2_Vs.model_caveat: Treat any simulation result involving SAMs qualitatively.

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.
interface_srv_cm_s.notes: 2PACz — SAM, low contact area but localized

2PACz.usage_note: Self-assembled monolayer (SAM). Bulk material parameters are a coarse approximation because SAMs are a few angstroms to ~1 nm thick. Treat simulations involving SAMs qualitatively.

Eg_eV.notes: Hossain 2022 Table 2, CBTS column. Tunable band gap chalcogenide.

thickness_nm_reference.notes: Reference thickness used in Hossain 2022 devices D1-D6.

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.

CBTS.usage_note: Champion HTL in Hossain 2022. Used in benchmark devices D1-D6 (CBTS-HTL combinations with PCBM/TiO2/ZnO/C60/IGZO/WS2 ETLs).

Eg_eV.notes: Hossain 2022 Table 2, CuI column. Wide-gap p-type.

thickness_nm_reference.notes: Chabri 2023 uses 100 nm CuI in optimized device D7.

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.

CuI.usage_note: Used in benchmark device D7 (ITO/CuI/Cs2SnI6/ZnO/AZO/Ag, Chabri 2023, PCE 14.65%). Material parameters cross-referenced from Hossain 2022 Table 2 (same parameter set widely used in SCAPS-1D community).

Eg_eV.notes: Hossain 2022 Table 2 CuSbS2 column. Direct optical bandgap.

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.

CuSbS2.usage_note: Inorganic Cu-based HTL. Narrower bandgap than CBTS but narrower CBO with lead-iodide perovskites can cause electron leakage; works best with CsPbI3 (1.69 eV) and lead-free Sn-based perovskites (1.3 eV).

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.

V2O5.usage_note: Wide-gap p-type oxide. Sometimes used as buffer between perovskite and silver/gold contact in inverted PSCs.

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.

P3HT.usage_note: Polymer HTL. Lower mobility than inorganic HTLs; popular for low-cost flexible PSCs.

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.

CFTS.usage_note: Cousin of CBTS. Narrower bandgap (1.3 eV) makes it parasitically absorbing — Hossain 2022 reports lower PCE than CBTS-based devices for this reason.

Eg_eV.notes: Wide bandgap p-type oxide; reported in multiple SCAPS-1D simulation papers including FAPbI3 device study.

interface_srv_cm_s.method: Effective surface recombination velocity at the perovskite-contact interface from time-resolved PL (Wang 2019 ACS Energy Lett.). Used to compute Voc deficit in fast_simulate.

SrCu2O2.usage_note: Used in published FAPbI3 SCAPS study with PCE 26.48% (FTO/PCBM/FAPbI3/SrCu2O2/Au).

CdTe-BSF.full_name: p+ CdTe back-surface layer (ohmic back-contact stand-in)

doping_cm3.method: heavy p+ doping approximating the low-barrier ohmic back contact of the baseline
doping_cm3.notes: The 2003 baseline models the back contact as a reduced barrier (~0.4 eV); a thin p+ layer is the standard 3-layer-stack equivalent used in later SCAPS studies

sigma_e_cm2.notes: With Nt=2e14 cm^-3 and v_th=1e7 cm/s gives tau_SRH = 0.5 ns, the community-baseline CdTe lifetime

changes: removed duplicate key yang_2015_nat_materials (0 params re-pointed to yang_2015_science)
separated measured vs device-effective mobility for 2 absorber params: MAPbI3/mu_n_cm2_Vs, MAPbI3/mu_p_cm2_Vs

_changelog.changes: Added CdTe technology family (CdTe absorber, CdS window, p+ CdTe back-surface layer) with Gloeckler 2003 baseline provenance; refs gloeckler_2003_wcpec, song_2016_vacuum, kephart_2016_solmat, ultrathin_cdte_2023_rg.
